---
title: "Introduction"
# draft: true    # uncomment to hide this chapter from the published site
---

## Motivation

Full prose explanation for the written script. This content appears here
only — not on the slides.

::: {.content-visible unless-format="revealjs"}
Extended derivation, background reading, detailed examples — script only.
:::

<!-- Teaching notes: outer div hides content unless --profile notes is passed -->
::: {.content-visible when-profile="notes"}
::: {.callout-tip}
## Teaching Note
Personal remarks visible only when rendering with `--profile notes`.
Use this for: pacing reminders, live-demo steps, common student questions,
board sketches to draw, references to previous semesters.
:::
:::

## Key Concept

Content that is relevant for both script and slides lives here as plain
markdown — no fencing needed.

$$
E = mc^2
$$ {#eq-example}

As shown in @eq-example ...

![Caption for a shared figure](../assets/diagrams/placeholder.svg){#fig-example}

See @fig-example for the circuit diagram.

<!-- shared content example: exercise or reference table -->
{{< include ../_shared/_exercise-example.qmd >}}

## Callout Reference

Quarto provides five callout types for drawing reader attention.
Delete this section once you no longer need the reference.

::: {.callout-note}
## Note
Supplementary information, definitions, or background reading.
:::

::: {.callout-tip}
## Tip
Best practices, shortcuts, or particularly useful insights.
:::

::: {.callout-warning}
## Warning
Common mistakes, pitfalls, or things that often cause confusion.
:::

::: {.callout-important}
## Important
Must-know facts — exam-relevant content or safety-critical information.
:::

::: {.callout-caution}
## Caution
Proceed carefully — edge cases, deprecated behavior, known traps.
:::

## Summary

- Point 1
- Point 2
- Point 3
